ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: Sex of subject at birth
Notes: M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: At your last visit, did you have a rapid STI test done, meaning you received Gonorrhea and Chlamydia test results during your visit? Rapid STI testing is a newer type of test for Gonorrhea and Chlamydia where you can get your test results during your visit, typically in about 45-60 minutes.
Notes: 0= No; 1= Yes

ElementDescription: For your STI test, did you provide a urine sample or a vaginal sample (a Q-tip or swab of fluid from the vagina)?
Notes: 0= Urine sample; 1= Vaginal sample; 2= I don't remember; 3= Not applicable (I don't have a vagina)

ElementDescription: How acceptable to you is having a vaginal sample collected by a doctor or nurse?
Notes: 0= Unacceptable; 1= Neither acceptable or unacceptable; 2= Acceptable; 3= Not applicable (I don't have a vagina)

ElementDescription: How acceptable to you is collecting a vaginal sample yourself?
Notes: 0= Unacceptable; 1= Neither acceptable or unacceptable; 2= Acceptable; 3= Not applicable (I don't have a vagina)

ElementDescription: How much, if at all, would you have preferred a rapid test over the regular lab testing you had at your visit?
Notes: 0= I prefer the test I had; 1= I would have preferred rapid a little; 2= No preference for either test; 3= I would have preferred the rapid test a lot

ElementDescription: How does the need for the vaginal sample change your preference for rapid testing?
Notes: 0= I prefer urine testing even if it takes longer to get results; 1= I would prefer a rapid testing vaginal swab a little over a urine test; 2= I have no preference for either test; 3= I would prefer a rapid testing vaginal swab a lot; 4= Not applicable (I don't have a vagina)

ElementDescription: Have you received your Gonorrhea and Chlamydia test results from your last visit?
Notes: 0= No; 1= Yes; 98= I don't remember

ElementDescription: How long (in minutes) did it take you to get your Gonorrhea and Chlamydia test results?
Notes: Number of minutes, from 30 minutes (1/2 hour) to 180 minutes (3 hours)

ElementDescription: What is the longest (in minutes) you would be able to wait in clinic for rapid test results?
Notes: 30= 30 minutes (1/2 hour); 60= 60 minutes (1 hour); 90= 90 minutes (1 1/2 hours); 120= 120 minutes (2 hours); 150= 150 minutes (2 1/2 hours); 180= 180 minutes (3 hours)

ElementDescription: How long (in days) did it take you to get your Gonorrhea and Chlamydia test results?
Notes: Number of days

ElementDescription: Please rate how interested you would be in receiving the services below while waiting for rapid STI testing results: Watching a 5-minute video on HIV and STI prevention.
Notes: 0= Not at all interested; 1= A little interested; 2= Somewhat interested; 3= Very interested

ElementDescription: Please rate how interested you would be in receiving the services below while waiting for rapid STI testing results: Getting a blood draw test for HIV (needle in your arm at the lab).
Notes: 0= Not at all interested; 1= A little interested; 2= Somewhat interested; 3= Very interested

ElementDescription: Please rate how interested you would be in receiving the services below while waiting for rapid STI testing results: Getting a fingerstick test for HIV.
Notes: 0= Not at all interested; 1= A little interested; 2= Somewhat interested; 3= Very interested

ElementDescription: Please rate how interested you would be in receiving the services below while waiting for rapid STI testing results: Getting a cheek swab test for HIV.
Notes: 0= Not at all interested; 1= A little interested; 2= Somewhat interested; 3= Very interested

ElementDescription: Please rate how interested you would be in receiving the services below while waiting for rapid STI testing results: Learning from your doctor or nurse about HIV and STI prevention, including HIV prevention medicine (PrEP).
Notes: 0= Not at all interested; 1= A little interested; 2= Somewhat interested; 3= Very interested

ElementDescription: Please rate how interested you would be in receiving the services below while waiting for rapid STI testing results: Talking to a health educator about HIV and STI prevention, including HIV prevention medicine (PrEP).
Notes: 0= Not at all interested; 1= A little interested; 2= Somewhat interested; 3= Very interested

ElementDescription: If a test showed you had Gonorrhea or Chlamydia during your visit, how likely would you be to get the following types of HIV tests during that visit: A blood draw test for HIV (needle in your arm at the lab).
Notes: 0= Not at all likely; 1= A little likely; 2= Somewhat likely; 3= Very likely

ElementDescription: If a test showed you had Gonorrhea or Chlamydia during your visit, how likely would you be to get the following types of HIV tests during that visit: A fingerstick test for HIV.
Notes: 0= Not at all likely; 1= A little likely; 2= Somewhat likely; 3= Very likely

ElementDescription: If a test showed you had Gonorrhea or Chlamydia during your visit, how likely would you be to get the following types of HIV tests during that visit: A cheek swab test for HIV.
Notes: 0= Not at all likely; 1= A little likely; 2= Somewhat likely; 3= Very likely

ElementDescription: If a test showed you had Gonorrhea or Chlamydia during your visit, how likely would you be to start PrEP (an HIV prevention medication) during that visit?
Notes: 0= Not at all likely; 1= A little likely; 2= Somewhat likely; 3= Very likely
